# SKILL.md — Embedded / Firmware / Hardware Control
## Industrial Grade | Zephyr RTOS + OpenOCD + QEMU | Deploy to STM32 / Pixhawk / Raspberry Pi

---

## INDUSTRY STANDARD STACK (what automotive, aerospace, defense actually uses)

| Layer | Tool | Industry Equivalent |
|---|---|---|
| RTOS | **Zephyr RTOS** | FreeRTOS, VxWorks, QNX |
| Build System | **CMake + West** | Yocto, Buildroot |
| Emulation | **QEMU** | Hardware prototyping |
| Debugger | **OpenOCD + GDB** | J-Link, TRACE32 |
| Firmware OTA | **MCUboot** | Automotive OTA (AUTOSAR) |
| Protocol | **CANopen / MAVLink** | Automotive CAN, DO-178 |
| Static Analysis | **cppcheck + clang-tidy** | Polyspace, Coverity |
| Unit Tests | **Unity + Zephyr Twister** | VectorCAST, LDRA |
| Hardware Target | **STM32F4 / Pixhawk / RPi** | NXP S32, Renesas RH850 |
| Python Bridge | **pyserial + MAVSDK** | AUTOSAR COM stack |

---

## PHASE 1 — ENVIRONMENT SETUP

### 1.1 Install Zephyr RTOS + West
```bash
# Install west (Zephyr's meta-tool)
pip install west

# Initialize Zephyr workspace
west init ~/zephyrproject
cd ~/zephyrproject
west update
west zephyr-export

# Install Python deps
pip install -r ~/zephyrproject/zephyr/scripts/requirements.txt

# Install Zephyr SDK (compiler, debugger, QEMU)
wget https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/sdk-ng/releases/download/v0.16.8/zephyr-sdk-0.16.8_linux-x86_64.tar.xz
tar xvf zephyr-sdk-0.16.8_linux-x86_64.tar.xz
cd zephyr-sdk-0.16.8
./setup.sh -t arm-zephyr-eabi     # ARM targets (STM32, Pixhawk)
./setup.sh -t x86_64-zephyr-elf   # QEMU x86 emulation
```

### 1.2 Install OpenOCD (Flash + Debug)
```bash
sudo apt-get install openocd -y
openocd --version
```

### 1.3 Install ARM GCC Toolchain
```bash
sudo apt-get install gcc-arm-none-eabi gdb-arm-none-eabi -y
arm-none-eabi-gcc --version
```

### 1.4 Install STM32CubeProgrammer (for STM32 flashing)
```bash
# Download from ST website (requires registration)
# https://www.st.com/en/development-tools/stm32cubeprog.html
# Extract and run installer
./SetupSTM32CubeProgrammer-2.15.0.linux
# Add to PATH
export PATH=$PATH:~/STMicroelectronics/STM32Cube/STM32CubeProgrammer/bin
```

### 1.5 Install QEMU (for hardware emulation without physical board)
```bash
sudo apt-get install qemu-system-arm qemu-system-misc -y
qemu-system-arm --version
```

---

## PHASE 2 — FIRMWARE DEVELOPMENT (Zephyr RTOS)

### 2.1 Project Structure
```
my_drone_firmware/
├── CMakeLists.txt
├── prj.conf          ← Kernel config (enable PWM, UART, I2C, etc.)
├── boards/           ← Custom board definitions
│   └── holybro_pixhawk6c.conf
└── src/
    ├── main.c        ← Entry point
    ├── flight_ctrl.c ← PID controller
    ├── imu_driver.c  ← IMU reading (ICM-42688)
    ├── esc_pwm.c     ← ESC PWM output
    └── telemetry.c   ← MAVLink serial output
```

### 2.2 Core Firmware — Flight Controller
Create `src/main.c`:
```c
/*
 * ProjectLauncher Compatible Firmware
 * Zephyr RTOS — Quadcopter Flight Controller Core
 * Target: STM32F4 / Pixhawk 6C
 * Industrial standard: DO-178C Level D compliant structure
 */
#include <zephyr/kernel.h>
#include <zephyr/device.h>
#include <zephyr/drivers/pwm.h>
#include <zephyr/drivers/sensor.h>
#include <zephyr/logging/log.h>

LOG_MODULE_REGISTER(flight_ctrl, LOG_LEVEL_INF);

/* ESC PWM channels — 1000-2000µs pulse width */
#define ESC_MIN_US   1000
#define ESC_MAX_US   2000
#define ESC_ARM_US   1000
#define PWM_PERIOD   20000  /* 50 Hz */

/* PID State */
typedef struct {
    float kp, ki, kd;
    float prev_error;
    float integral;
} pid_t;

/* Attitude Setpoints */
static float target_roll  = 0.0f;
static float target_pitch = 0.0f;
static float target_yaw   = 0.0f;
static float throttle     = 0.0f;

static pid_t roll_pid  = {.kp=6.5f, .ki=0.5f, .kd=0.3f};
static pid_t pitch_pid = {.kp=6.5f, .ki=0.5f, .kd=0.3f};
static pid_t yaw_pid   = {.kp=2.8f, .ki=0.1f, .kd=0.1f};

float pid_compute(pid_t *pid, float setpoint, float measured, float dt) {
    float error    = setpoint - measured;
    pid->integral += error * dt;
    pid->integral  = CLAMP(pid->integral, -100.0f, 100.0f); /* anti-windup */
    float deriv    = (error - pid->prev_error) / dt;
    pid->prev_error = error;
    return pid->kp*error + pid->ki*pid->integral + pid->kd*deriv;
}

/* Motor mixing — quadcopter X-frame
 *    M1(CW)   M2(CCW)
 *       \     /
 *        \   /
 *    M3(CCW) M4(CW)
 */
void motor_mix(float throttle, float roll, float pitch, float yaw,
               float *m1, float *m2, float *m3, float *m4) {
    *m1 = throttle - roll + pitch - yaw;  /* front-left */
    *m2 = throttle + roll + pitch + yaw;  /* front-right */
    *m3 = throttle - roll - pitch + yaw;  /* rear-left */
    *m4 = throttle + roll - pitch - yaw;  /* rear-right */
    /* Clamp to valid range */
    *m1 = CLAMP(*m1, 0.0f, 100.0f);
    *m2 = CLAMP(*m2, 0.0f, 100.0f);
    *m3 = CLAMP(*m3, 0.0f, 100.0f);
    *m4 = CLAMP(*m4, 0.0f, 100.0f);
}

void flight_control_thread(void *p1, void *p2, void *p3) {
    /* Get IMU device */
    const struct device *imu = DEVICE_DT_GET(DT_NODELABEL(imu0));
    if (!device_is_ready(imu)) {
        LOG_ERR("IMU not ready");
        return;
    }

    struct sensor_value accel[3], gyro[3];
    float roll_actual, pitch_actual, yaw_actual;
    float m1, m2, m3, m4;
    const float dt = 0.004f; /* 250 Hz control loop */

    LOG_INF("Flight controller started at 250Hz");

    while (1) {
        /* Read IMU */
        sensor_sample_fetch(imu);
        sensor_channel_get(imu, SENSOR_CHAN_ACCEL_XYZ, accel);
        sensor_channel_get(imu, SENSOR_CHAN_GYRO_XYZ, gyro);

        roll_actual  = sensor_value_to_float(&gyro[0]);
        pitch_actual = sensor_value_to_float(&gyro[1]);
        yaw_actual   = sensor_value_to_float(&gyro[2]);

        /* PID compute */
        float roll_out  = pid_compute(&roll_pid,  target_roll,  roll_actual,  dt);
        float pitch_out = pid_compute(&pitch_pid, target_pitch, pitch_actual, dt);
        float yaw_out   = pid_compute(&yaw_pid,   target_yaw,   yaw_actual,   dt);

        /* Motor mixing */
        motor_mix(throttle, roll_out, pitch_out, yaw_out, &m1, &m2, &m3, &m4);

        /* Output to ESCs via PWM */
        /* pwm_set(...) calls here for each motor */

        LOG_DBG("M1:%.1f M2:%.1f M3:%.1f M4:%.1f", m1, m2, m3, m4);

        k_sleep(K_USEC(4000)); /* 250Hz = 4ms */
    }
}

K_THREAD_DEFINE(flight_tid, 4096, flight_control_thread, NULL, NULL, NULL, 5, 0, 0);

int main(void) {
    LOG_INF("ProjectLauncher Drone Firmware v1.0 starting...");
    LOG_INF("Target: Pixhawk 6C | RTOS: Zephyr");
    return 0;
}
```

### 2.3 Kernel Config — `prj.conf`
```ini
# Flight controller kernel config
CONFIG_PWM=y
CONFIG_SENSOR=y
CONFIG_I2C=y
CONFIG_SPI=y
CONFIG_UART_INTERRUPT_DRIVEN=y
CONFIG_LOG=y
CONFIG_LOG_DEFAULT_LEVEL=3
CONFIG_THREAD_STACK_INFO=y
CONFIG_MAIN_STACK_SIZE=4096
CONFIG_SYSTEM_WORKQUEUE_STACK_SIZE=2048
# MAVLink serial
CONFIG_SERIAL=y
CONFIG_UART_CONSOLE=y
# Safety watchdog
CONFIG_WATCHDOG=y
CONFIG_WDT_DISABLE_AT_BOOT=n
```

### 2.4 CMakeLists.txt
```cmake
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.20.0)
find_package(Zephyr REQUIRED HINTS $ENV{ZEPHYR_BASE})
project(drone_firmware)

target_sources(app PRIVATE
    src/main.c
    src/flight_ctrl.c
    src/imu_driver.c
    src/esc_pwm.c
    src/telemetry.c
)
```

---

## PHASE 3 — BUILD & EMULATE (No Hardware Needed)

### 3.1 Build for QEMU (test without hardware)
```bash
cd ~/zephyrproject/zephyr
west build -b qemu_cortex_m3 path/to/my_drone_firmware -- -DCONF_FILE=prj.conf
west build -t run   # runs in QEMU emulator
```

Expected output:
```
[00:00:00.000] Flight controller started at 250Hz
[00:00:00.004] M1:50.0 M2:50.0 M3:50.0 M4:50.0
```

### 3.2 Build for STM32F4 (Nucleo board / Pixhawk)
```bash
west build -b nucleo_f401re path/to/my_drone_firmware
# Output: build/zephyr/zephyr.bin
```

### 3.3 Build for Pixhawk 6C
```bash
# Pixhawk uses NuttX but also runs Zephyr on companion processors
# For STM32H7 (Pixhawk 6C FMU chip):
west build -b fmu-v6c path/to/my_drone_firmware
```

---

## PHASE 4 — FLASH TO REAL HARDWARE

### 4.1 Flash via OpenOCD (STM32 + ST-Link)
```bash
# Build first
west build -b nucleo_f401re .

# Flash
west flash
# OR directly with OpenOCD:
openocd -f interface/stlink.cfg \
        -f target/stm32f4x.cfg \
        -c "program build/zephyr/zephyr.bin verify reset exit 0x08000000"
```

### 4.2 Flash via STM32CubeProgrammer (GUI)
```bash
STM32_Programmer_CLI -c port=SWD -d build/zephyr/zephyr.bin 0x08000000 -v -rst
```

### 4.3 Flash Pixhawk via QGroundControl
```
QGroundControl → Vehicle Setup → Firmware → Custom firmware → browse to zephyr.bin
```

### 4.4 Monitor Serial Output
```bash
# Linux
screen /dev/ttyACM0 115200
# OR
minicom -D /dev/ttyACM0 -b 115200

# Windows
# Use PuTTY → Serial → COM3 → 115200
```

---

## PHASE 5 — STATIC ANALYSIS & TESTING (Industrial Certification)

### 5.1 Static Analysis (cppcheck)
```bash
pip install cppcheck
cppcheck --enable=all --std=c11 \
         --suppress=missingIncludeSystem \
         --xml --xml-version=2 \
         src/ 2> cppcheck_report.xml
echo "✅ Static analysis complete → cppcheck_report.xml"
```

### 5.2 Unit Tests (Zephyr Twister)
```bash
cd ~/zephyrproject/zephyr
./scripts/twister -T path/to/my_drone_firmware/tests \
                  -p qemu_cortex_m3 \
                  --build-only

# Run tests
./scripts/twister -T path/to/my_drone_firmware/tests \
                  -p qemu_cortex_m3

# Check results
cat twister-out/twister.json | python3 -m json.tool | grep "passed\|failed"
```

### 5.3 Hardware-in-the-Loop Validation (Python)
```python
# hitl_validate.py — validates firmware on real hardware via serial
import serial, time, json

PORT = "/dev/ttyACM0"   # Windows: "COM3"
BAUD = 115200

def validate_firmware():
    results = {}
    with serial.Serial(PORT, BAUD, timeout=5) as ser:
        time.sleep(2)  # wait for boot
        ser.write(b"STATUS\n")
        response = ser.readline().decode().strip()

        results["boot"] = "starting" in response.lower() or len(response) > 0
        results["imu_active"] = "M1:" in response
        results["frequency_ok"] = True  # validate 250Hz timing

    passed = all(results.values())
    print(f"\n=== FIRMWARE VALIDATION ===")
    for k, v in results.items():
        print(f"  {'✅' if v else '❌'} {k}")
    print(f"\nResult: {'✅ FIRMWARE VALIDATED' if passed else '❌ VALIDATION FAILED'}")
    with open("firmware_validation.json", "w") as f:
        json.dump(results, f, indent=2)

validate_firmware()
```

---

## INTEGRATION WITH PROJECTLAUNCHER

When ProjectLauncher scans an embedded firmware project:
- Detects `CMakeLists.txt` + `prj.conf` → identifies as Zephyr RTOS project
- `requirements.txt` includes: `pyserial`, `west`, `intelhex`
- **Install Requirements** button: runs `pip install west pyserial` + `west update`
- **Start Project** button: runs `west build -b qemu_cortex_m3 . && west build -t run`
- AI scanner: `projectType: "zephyr-rtos"`, `startCommand: "west build -b qemu_cortex_m3 . && west build -t run"`
- Terminal streams QEMU/hardware serial output live
- For physical flash: Start button detects connected USB and runs `west flash`

97	```python
98	"""
99	Custom SLAM validation node
100	Subscribes to /odom and /scan, publishes map quality metrics
101	"""
102	import rclpy
103	from rclpy.node import Node
104	from nav_msgs.msg import Odometry, OccupancyGrid
105	from sensor_msgs.msg import LaserScan
106	import numpy as np
107	import json
108	
109	class SLAMValidator(Node):
110	    def __init__(self):
111	        super().__init__('slam_validator')
112	        self.odom_sub = self.create_subscription(Odometry, '/odom', self.odom_cb, 10)
113	        self.scan_sub = self.create_subscription(LaserScan, '/scan', self.scan_cb, 10)
114	        self.map_sub  = self.create_subscription(OccupancyGrid, '/map', self.map_cb, 10)
115	
116	        self.positions = []
117	        self.scan_count = 0
118	        self.map_cells_explored = 0
119	        self.timer = self.create_timer(5.0, self.report)
120	        self.get_logger().info("SLAM Validator started — monitoring map quality")
121	
122	    def odom_cb(self, msg):
123	        x = msg.pose.pose.position.x
124	        y = msg.pose.pose.position.y
125	        self.positions.append((x, y))
126	
127	    def scan_cb(self, msg):
128	        self.scan_count += 1
129	        valid = [r for r in msg.ranges if msg.range_min < r < msg.range_max]
130	        # Log scan quality
131	        if self.scan_count % 100 == 0:
132	            self.get_logger().info(f"Scan #{self.scan_count}: {len(valid)}/{len(msg.ranges)} valid rays")
133	
134	    def map_cb(self, msg):
135	        data = np.array(msg.data)
136	        self.map_cells_explored = int(np.sum(data >= 0))  # non-unknown cells
137	
138	    def report(self):
139	        if not self.positions:
140	            return
141	        positions = np.array(self.positions)
142	        dist_travelled = np.sum(np.linalg.norm(np.diff(positions, axis=0), axis=1))
143	
144	        metrics = {
145	            "distance_travelled_m":    round(float(dist_travelled), 3),
146	            "map_cells_explored":      self.map_cells_explored,
147	            "total_scans_processed":   self.scan_count,
148	            "estimated_area_m2":       round(self.map_cells_explored * 0.05**2, 2),  # 5cm resolution
149	            "localization_status":     "✅ ACTIVE" if self.scan_count > 10 else "⚠️  INITIALIZING",
150	        }
151	        self.get_logger().info(f"\n=== SLAM METRICS ===\n{json.dumps(metrics, indent=2)}")
152	        with open("slam_metrics.json", "w") as f:
153	            json.dump(metrics, f, indent=2)
154	
155	def main():
156	    rclpy.init()
157	    node = SLAMValidator()
158	    rclpy.spin(node)
159	    node.destroy_node()
160	    rclpy.shutdown()
161	
162	if __name__ == '__main__':
163	    main()
164	```

231	```python
232	"""
233	Industrial robot arm path planner using MoveIt2
234	Runs in simulation first, then identical code deploys to real arm
235	"""
236	import rclpy
237	from rclpy.node import Node
238	from moveit.planning import MoveItPy
239	from moveit.core.robot_state import RobotState
240	from geometry_msgs.msg import Pose, Point, Quaternion
241	import numpy as np
242	
243	class ArmPlanner(Node):
244	    def __init__(self):
245	        super().__init__('arm_planner')
246	        self.moveit = MoveItPy(node_name="arm_planner")
247	        self.arm = self.moveit.get_planning_component("arm_group")
248	        self.get_logger().info("MoveIt2 arm planner initialized")
249	
250	    def move_to_pose(self, x, y, z, roll=0, pitch=0, yaw=0):
251	        """Plan and execute move to Cartesian pose"""
252	        self.get_logger().info(f"Planning move to ({x:.3f}, {y:.3f}, {z:.3f})")
253	
254	        # Set target pose
255	        pose_goal = Pose()
256	        pose_goal.position.x = x
257	        pose_goal.position.y = y
258	        pose_goal.position.z = z
259	        # Simple orientation (identity quaternion)
260	        pose_goal.orientation.w = 1.0
261	
262	        self.arm.set_start_state_to_current_state()
263	        self.arm.set_goal_state(pose_stamped_msg=pose_goal,
264	                                 pose_link="end_effector")
265	
266	        # Plan
267	        plan_result = self.arm.plan()
268	        if plan_result:
269	            robot_traj = plan_result.trajectory
270	            self.get_logger().info("✅ Path planned successfully")
271	
272	            # Validate trajectory
273	            self.validate_trajectory(robot_traj)
274	
275	            # Execute
276	            self.moveit.execute(robot_traj, controllers=[])
277	            self.get_logger().info("✅ Motion executed")
278	            return True
279	        else:
280	            self.get_logger().error("❌ Planning failed — check workspace limits")
281	            return False
282	
283	    def validate_trajectory(self, traj):
284	        """Industrial validation — check velocity/acceleration limits"""
285	        points = traj.joint_trajectory.points
286	        self.get_logger().info(f"Trajectory: {len(points)} waypoints")
287	        max_vel = max(max(abs(v) for v in p.velocities) for p in points if p.velocities)
288	        self.get_logger().info(f"Max joint velocity: {max_vel:.3f} rad/s")
289	        if max_vel > 1.5:
290	            self.get_logger().warning("⚠️  High velocity — check safety limits")
291	
292	    def inspection_sequence(self):
293	        """Full drone inspection arm sequence"""
294	        self.get_logger().info("=== STARTING INSPECTION SEQUENCE ===")
295	
296	        waypoints = [
297	            (0.20, 0.00, 0.30),  # ready position
298	            (0.30, 0.10, 0.25),  # inspect point 1
299	            (0.30, -0.10, 0.25), # inspect point 2
300	            (0.25, 0.00, 0.35),  # inspect point 3
301	            (0.20, 0.00, 0.30),  # back to ready
302	        ]
303	
304	        for i, (x, y, z) in enumerate(waypoints):
305	            self.get_logger().info(f"Waypoint {i+1}/{len(waypoints)}")
306	            success = self.move_to_pose(x, y, z)
307	            if not success:
308	                self.get_logger().error(f"Failed at waypoint {i+1} — ABORT")
309	                return False
310	
311	        self.get_logger().info("✅ INSPECTION SEQUENCE COMPLETE")
312	        return True
313	
314	def main():
315	    rclpy.init()
316	    node = ArmPlanner()
317	    node.inspection_sequence()
318	    rclpy.shutdown()
319	
320	if __name__ == '__main__':
321	    main()
322	```

354	### 4.2 Navigation Goal Script
355	Create `nav_goal.py`:
356	```python
357	"""
358	Send autonomous navigation goals via Nav2
359	Works in simulation AND on real robot (change use_sim_time)
360	"""
361	import rclpy
362	from rclpy.node import Node
363	from rclpy.action import ActionClient
364	from nav2_msgs.action import NavigateToPose
365	from geometry_msgs.msg import PoseStamped
366	import time
367	
368	class Navigator(Node):
369	    def __init__(self):
370	        super().__init__('navigator')
371	        self._client = ActionClient(self, NavigateToPose, 'navigate_to_pose')
372	
373	    def send_goal(self, x, y, yaw=0.0):
374	        self.get_logger().info(f"Navigating to ({x}, {y})...")

# SKILL.md — Robotics / Arm / SLAM
## Industrial Grade | ROS2 + Nav2 + MoveIt2 + Gazebo | Deploy to Real Robot / Drone

---

## INDUSTRY STANDARD STACK (what Boston Dynamics, ABB, NASA JPL actually use)

| Layer | Tool | Industry Equivalent |
|---|---|---|
| Middleware | **ROS2 Humble** | Custom DDS middleware |
| Arm Planning | **MoveIt2** | ABB RobotStudio, KUKA.Sim |
| Navigation | **Nav2** | Autonomy stacks in AMRs |
| SLAM | **RTAB-Map / Cartographer** | Leica, FARO 3D mapping |
| Simulation | **Gazebo Garden** | Webots, Isaac Sim |
| Perception | **OpenCV + PCL** | Cognex, Sick LiDAR SDK |
| Robot Model | **URDF / XACRO** | Standard across all ROS robots |
| Localization | **AMCL + EKF** | Kalman filter fusion (industry) |
| Fleet Mgmt | **ROS2 Fleet** | AWS RoboMaker, MassRobotics |
| Hardware | **Raspberry Pi 4 + Arduino** | NUC + STM32 (common combo) |

---

## PHASE 1 — ENVIRONMENT SETUP

### 1.1 Install ROS2 Humble (Ubuntu 22.04)
```bash
sudo apt install software-properties-common curl -y
sudo curl -sSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ros/rosdistro/master/ros.key \
  -o /usr/share/keyrings/ros-archive-keyring.gpg
echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/ros-archive-keyring.gpg] \
  http://packages.ros.org/ros2/ubuntu jammy main" \
  | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/ros2.list
sudo apt update
sudo apt install ros-humble-desktop ros-dev-tools -y
echo "source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash" >> ~/.bashrc
source ~/.bashrc
```

### 1.2 Install MoveIt2 (Robot Arm Planning)
```bash
sudo apt install ros-humble-moveit ros-humble-moveit-ros-planning-interface -y
sudo apt install ros-humble-moveit-visual-tools -y
```

### 1.3 Install Nav2 (Autonomous Navigation)
```bash
sudo apt install ros-humble-navigation2 ros-humble-nav2-bringup -y
sudo apt install ros-humble-slam-toolbox ros-humble-rtabmap-ros -y
```

### 1.4 Install Gazebo Garden + ROS2 Bridge
```bash
sudo apt-get install ros-humble-ros-gz -y
sudo apt-get install ros-humble-gazebo-ros-pkgs -y
```

### 1.5 Install Additional Perception Tools
```bash
pip install opencv-python-headless open3d numpy scipy
sudo apt install ros-humble-pcl-ros ros-humble-sensor-msgs -y
```

---

## PHASE 2 — SLAM (Simultaneous Localization and Mapping)

### 2.1 SLAM with RTAB-Map (industry-grade 3D SLAM)

```bash
# Launch SLAM in simulation
ros2 launch rtabmap_demos turtlebot3_sim_scan.launch.py

# OR launch with a real LiDAR (RPLiDAR A1/A2)
ros2 launch rtabmap_ros rtabmap.launch.py \
    rtabmap_args:="--delete_db_on_start" \
    rgb_topic:=/camera/rgb/image_raw \
    depth_topic:=/camera/depth_registered/image_raw \
    camera_info_topic:=/camera/rgb/camera_info \
    scan_topic:=/scan
```

### 2.2 2D SLAM with Slam Toolbox (lighter weight)
```bash
# Terminal 1: Start Slam Toolbox
ros2 launch slam_toolbox online_async_launch.py

# Terminal 2: Drive robot around to build map
ros2 run teleop_twist_keyboard teleop_twist_keyboard

# Terminal 3: Save the map when done
ros2 run nav2_map_server map_saver_cli -f ~/my_map
# Creates: my_map.pgm + my_map.yaml
```

### 2.3 SLAM Python Node — Custom Localization
Create `slam_node.py`:
```python
"""
Custom SLAM validation node
Subscribes to /odom and /scan, publishes map quality metrics
"""
import rclpy
from rclpy.node import Node
from nav_msgs.msg import Odometry, OccupancyGrid
from sensor_msgs.msg import LaserScan
import numpy as np
import json

class SLAMValidator(Node):
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__('slam_validator')
        self.odom_sub = self.create_subscription(Odometry, '/odom', self.odom_cb, 10)
        self.scan_sub = self.create_subscription(LaserScan, '/scan', self.scan_cb, 10)
        self.map_sub  = self.create_subscription(OccupancyGrid, '/map', self.map_cb, 10)

        self.positions = []
        self.scan_count = 0
        self.map_cells_explored = 0
        self.timer = self.create_timer(5.0, self.report)
        self.get_logger().info("SLAM Validator started — monitoring map quality")

    def odom_cb(self, msg):
        x = msg.pose.pose.position.x
        y = msg.pose.pose.position.y
        self.positions.append((x, y))

    def scan_cb(self, msg):
        self.scan_count += 1
        valid = [r for r in msg.ranges if msg.range_min < r < msg.range_max]
        # Log scan quality
        if self.scan_count % 100 == 0:
            self.get_logger().info(f"Scan #{self.scan_count}: {len(valid)}/{len(msg.ranges)} valid rays")

    def map_cb(self, msg):
        data = np.array(msg.data)
        self.map_cells_explored = int(np.sum(data >= 0))  # non-unknown cells

    def report(self):
        if not self.positions:
            return
        positions = np.array(self.positions)
        dist_travelled = np.sum(np.linalg.norm(np.diff(positions, axis=0), axis=1))

        metrics = {
            "distance_travelled_m":    round(float(dist_travelled), 3),
            "map_cells_explored":      self.map_cells_explored,
            "total_scans_processed":   self.scan_count,
            "estimated_area_m2":       round(self.map_cells_explored * 0.05**2, 2),  # 5cm resolution
            "localization_status":     "✅ ACTIVE" if self.scan_count > 10 else "⚠️  INITIALIZING",
        }
        self.get_logger().info(f"\n=== SLAM METRICS ===\n{json.dumps(metrics, indent=2)}")
        with open("slam_metrics.json", "w") as f:
            json.dump(metrics, f, indent=2)

def main():
    rclpy.init()
    node = SLAMValidator()
    rclpy.spin(node)
    node.destroy_node()
    rclpy.shutdown()

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
```

Run it:
```bash
ros2 run my_robot_pkg slam_node
```

## PHASE 3 — ROBOT ARM (MoveIt2 Motion Planning)

### 3.1 Create URDF for a 6-DOF Arm

Create `urdf/robot_arm.urdf.xacro`:
```xml

  <!-- Add joints 2-6 similarly... -->
  <!-- End effector -->
```

### 3.2 MoveIt2 Motion Planning (Python)
Create `arm_planner.py`:
```python
"""
Industrial robot arm path planner using MoveIt2
Runs in simulation first, then identical code deploys to real arm
"""
import rclpy
from rclpy.node import Node
from moveit.planning import MoveItPy
from moveit.core.robot_state import RobotState
from geometry_msgs.msg import Pose, Point, Quaternion
import numpy as np

class ArmPlanner(Node):
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__('arm_planner')
        self.moveit = MoveItPy(node_name="arm_planner")
        self.arm = self.moveit.get_planning_component("arm_group")
        self.get_logger().info("MoveIt2 arm planner initialized")

    def move_to_pose(self, x, y, z, roll=0, pitch=0, yaw=0):
        """Plan and execute move to Cartesian pose"""
        self.get_logger().info(f"Planning move to ({x:.3f}, {y:.3f}, {z:.3f})")

        # Set target pose
        pose_goal = Pose()
        pose_goal.position.x = x
        pose_goal.position.y = y
        pose_goal.position.z = z
        # Simple orientation (identity quaternion)
        pose_goal.orientation.w = 1.0

        self.arm.set_start_state_to_current_state()
        self.arm.set_goal_state(pose_stamped_msg=pose_goal,
                                 pose_link="end_effector")

        # Plan
        plan_result = self.arm.plan()
        if plan_result:
            robot_traj = plan_result.trajectory
            self.get_logger().info("✅ Path planned successfully")

            # Validate trajectory
            self.validate_trajectory(robot_traj)

            # Execute
            self.moveit.execute(robot_traj, controllers=[])
            self.get_logger().info("✅ Motion executed")
            return True
        else:
            self.get_logger().error("❌ Planning failed — check workspace limits")
            return False

    def validate_trajectory(self, traj):
        """Industrial validation — check velocity/acceleration limits"""
        points = traj.joint_trajectory.points
        self.get_logger().info(f"Trajectory: {len(points)} waypoints")
        max_vel = max(max(abs(v) for v in p.velocities) for p in points if p.velocities)
        self.get_logger().info(f"Max joint velocity: {max_vel:.3f} rad/s")
        if max_vel > 1.5:
            self.get_logger().warning("⚠️  High velocity — check safety limits")

    def inspection_sequence(self):
        """Full drone inspection arm sequence"""
        self.get_logger().info("=== STARTING INSPECTION SEQUENCE ===")

        waypoints = [
            (0.20, 0.00, 0.30),  # ready position
            (0.30, 0.10, 0.25),  # inspect point 1
            (0.30, -0.10, 0.25), # inspect point 2
            (0.25, 0.00, 0.35),  # inspect point 3
            (0.20, 0.00, 0.30),  # back to ready
        ]

        for i, (x, y, z) in enumerate(waypoints):
            self.get_logger().info(f"Waypoint {i+1}/{len(waypoints)}")
            success = self.move_to_pose(x, y, z)
            if not success:
                self.get_logger().error(f"Failed at waypoint {i+1} — ABORT")
                return False

        self.get_logger().info("✅ INSPECTION SEQUENCE COMPLETE")
        return True

def main():
    rclpy.init()
    node = ArmPlanner()
    node.inspection_sequence()
    rclpy.shutdown()

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
```

```bash
# Terminal 2: Launch MoveIt2
```

## PHASE 4 — AUTONOMOUS NAVIGATION (Nav2)

### 4.1 Launch Nav2 with Existing Map
```bash
# Terminal 1: Launch Gazebo
ros2 launch nav2_bringup tb3_simulation_launch.py

# Terminal 2: Nav2 with saved map
ros2 launch nav2_bringup navigation_launch.py \
    map:=$HOME/my_map.yaml \
    use_sim_time:=True

# Terminal 3: Send navigation goal via Python
python3 nav_goal.py
```

### 4.2 Navigation Goal Script
Create `nav_goal.py`:
```python
"""
Send autonomous navigation goals via Nav2
Works in simulation AND on real robot (change use_sim_time)
"""
import rclpy
from rclpy.node import Node
from rclpy.action import ActionClient
from nav2_msgs.action import NavigateToPose
from geometry_msgs.msg import PoseStamped
import time

class Navigator(Node):
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__('navigator')
        self._client = ActionClient(self, NavigateToPose, 'navigate_to_pose')

    def send_goal(self, x, y, yaw=0.0):
        self.get_logger().info(f"Navigating to ({x}, {y})...")
        self._client.wait_for_server()

        goal = NavigateToPose.Goal()
        goal.pose.header.frame_id = 'map'
        goal.pose.header.stamp = self.get_clock().now().to_msg()
        goal.pose.pose.position.x = float(x)
        goal.pose.pose.position.y = float(y)
        goal.pose.pose.orientation.z = float(yaw)
        goal.pose.pose.orientation.w = 1.0

        future = self._client.send_goal_async(goal)
        rclpy.spin_until_future_complete(self, future)
        result = future.result()

        if result.accepted:
            self.get_logger().info("✅ Goal accepted — navigating...")
            result_future = result.get_result_async()
            rclpy.spin_until_future_complete(self, result_future)
            self.get_logger().info("✅ Goal reached!")
            return True
        else:
            self.get_logger().error("❌ Goal rejected")
            return False

def main():
    rclpy.init()
    nav = Navigator()

    # Patrol route
    waypoints = [(1.0, 0.0), (1.0, 1.0), (0.0, 1.0), (0.0, 0.0)]
    for x, y in waypoints:
        success = nav.send_goal(x, y)
        if not success:
            break

    nav.destroy_node()
    rclpy.shutdown()

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
```

```python
# test_robotics.py

def run_test(name, cmd, expected_in_output):

    passed = expected_in_output in result.stdout or expected_in_output in result.stderr

run_test("ROS2 available",    "ros2 --version", "humble")

run_test("Nav2 installed",    "ros2 pkg list | grep navigation2", "navigation2")

          "passed": sum(1 for t in tests if t["passed"]),
          "total": len(tests)}

with open("robotics_validation.json", "w") as f:
    json.dump(report, f, indent=2)

print(f"\n{report['passed']}/{report['total']} tests passed")
print(f"Report → robotics_validation.json")
```

## INTEGRATION WITH PROJECTLAUNCHER

When ProjectLauncher scans a ROS2 project:
- Detects `package.xml` + `CMakeLists.txt` → identifies as ROS2 package
- Detects `setup.py` → Python ROS2 package
- `requirements.txt` includes: `rclpy`, `numpy`, `scipy`, `open3d`
- **Install Requirements** button: runs `pip install -r requirements.txt` + `colcon build`
- **Start Project** button: runs `ros2 launch <package> <main_launch_file>.launch.py`
- AI scanner: `projectType: "ros2-robotics"`, `startCommand: "ros2 launch my_pkg main.launch.py"`
- Terminal streams all ROS2 node logs live with color coding
- `/info` and `/error` log levels color-coded (green/yellow/red) in terminal panel

- Start: `west build -b qemu_cortex_m3 . && west build -t run`
- AI: `projectType: "zephyr-rtos"`
- Serial output streaming

From SKILL (10) - Robotics/Arm/SLAM:
- Detect ROS2 packages (package.xml + CMakeLists.txt)
- Install: `pip install -r requirements.txt` + `colcon build`
- Start: `ros2 launch <package> <launch_file>.launch.py`
- AI: `projectType: "ros2-robotics"`
- ROS2 log level color coding

From SKILL (11) - Structural/FEM/CFD:
- Detect FEM/CFD projects
- Install: `fenics-dolfinx`, `gmsh`, `meshio`, `pyvista`, `matplotlib`
- AI: `projectType: "fem-cfd"`, `startCommand: "python drone_arm_fea.py"`
- Terminal streams solver iterations

So "industrial grade" improvements needed:

1. **Expanded heuristic analysis** - Detect all these project types (PX4, Zephyr, ROS2, FEM/CFD)
2. **Expanded package manager detection** - cargo, poetry, pipenv, colcon, west
3. **Better command resolution** - ROS2 launch, west build, colcon, etc.
4. **Output color coding** - ROS2 log levels, MAVLink messages, solver iterations
5. **Error handling robustness** - Better subprocess management, timeout handling
6. **Project type badges** - Add new types: px4-uav, zephyr-rtos, ros2-robotics, fem-cfd
7. **TypeScript migration** - For proper type safety
8. **Better state management** - More robust error handling in IPC
9. **Validation/test framework** - Automated validation scripts

Let me focus on the most impactful changes to the existing codebase:

1. **Update `electron/main.cjs`** - Enhanced scanner for all project types, better package manager detection
2. **Update `AIScanner.jsx`** - Enhanced heuristic analysis for all project types
3. **Update `ProjectInfo.jsx`** - Badges for all new project types
4. **Update `ActionButtons.jsx`** - Enhanced command resolution for ROS2, west, colcon, etc.
5. **Update `Terminal.jsx`** - Color coding for ROS2/MAVLink/solver log levels

Let me create a task list and start implementing.

Self-Learning Hooks System

Intelligent workflow automation with pattern learning and adaptive routing

Usage: claude-flow hooks <subcommand> [options]

Subcommands:
  - pre-edit        - Get context before editing files
  - post-edit       - Record editing outcomes for learning
  - pre-command     - Assess risk before executing commands
  - post-command    - Record command execution outcomes
  - pre-task        - Record task start and get agent suggestions
  - post-task       - Record task completion for learning
  - session-end     - End current session and persist state
  - session-restore - Restore a previous session
  - route           - Route tasks to optimal agents
  - explain         - Explain routing decisions
  - pretrain        - Bootstrap intelligence from repository
  - build-agents    - Generate optimized agent configs
  - metrics         - View learning metrics dashboard
  - transfer        - Transfer patterns from another project
  - list            - List all registered hooks
  - worker          - Background worker management (12 workers)
  - progress        - Check V3 implementation progress
  - statusline      - Generate dynamic statusline display
  - coverage-route  - Route tasks based on coverage gaps (ruvector)
  - coverage-suggest- Suggest coverage improvements
  - coverage-gaps   - List all coverage gaps with agents
  - token-optimize - Token optimization (agentic-flow integration)
  - model-route    - Route to optimal model (haiku/sonnet/opus)
  - model-outcome  - Record model routing outcome
  - model-stats    - View model routing statistics
  - 
  - Agent Teams:
  - teammate-idle  - Handle idle teammate (auto-assign tasks)
  - task-completed - Handle task completion (train patterns)

Run "claude-flow hooks <subcommand> --help" for subcommand help

V3 Features:
  - 🧠 ReasoningBank adaptive learning
  - ⚡ Flash Attention (2.49x-7.47x speedup)
  - 🔍 AgentDB integration (150x faster search)
  - 📊 84.8% SWE-Bench solve rate
  - 🎯 32.3% token reduction
  - 🚀 2.8-4.4x speed improvement
  - 👥 Agent Teams integration (auto task assignment)

content.text:
App rebuilt and running. Here's everything that was upgraded:

---

## Industrial-Grade Upgrades Applied

### 1. Electron Scanner (`electron/main.cjs`) — 8+ Project Types

The file scanner now detects:

| Domain | Detection Markers | Project Type |
|---|---|---|
| **ROS2 Robotics** | `package.xml` + `CMakeLists.txt` + `launch/*.py` | `ros2-robotics` |
| **PX4 / Drone** | `mission_test.py`, `preflight_check.py`, MAVSDK | `px4-uav` |
| **Zephyr RTOS** | `prj.conf`, `west.yml`, `main.c` | `zephyr-rtos` |
| **FEM / CFD** | `drone_arm_fea.py`, `cfd_report.py`, FEniCSx | `fem-cfd` |
| **Rust / Cargo** | `Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.lock` | `rust-cargo` |
| **Poetry** | `pyproject.toml` + `poetry.lock` | poetry |
| **Pipenv** | `Pipfile`, `Pipfile.lock` | pipenv |
| **Yarn** | `yarn.lock` | yarn |
| **Conan** | `conanfile.txt` | conan |

Also detects **ROS2 launch files** (`.launch.py`, `.launch.xml`) and **build systems** (`colcon`, `west`, `cmake`). Scan depth increased to 5 levels.

### 2. AI/Heuristic Engine (`AIScanner.jsx`)

- **AI prompt** now describes all 8 industrial domains to the model
- **Validates** returned `projectType` against known types, falls back to heuristic if hallucinated
- **Heuristic analysis** expanded with priority-ordered detection for all domains:
  - ROS2 → colcon build, ros2 launch
  - PX4 → `make px4_sitl`, `python mission_test.py`
  - Zephyr → `west build -b qemu_cortex_m3`, `west build -t run`
  - FEM/CFD → `python drone_arm_fea.py`, fenics-dolfinx install
  - Rust → `cargo build`, `cargo run`
  - Plus all existing web types (Django/Flask/FastAPI/Next/Vite/Streamlit)

### 3. Project Type Badges (`ProjectInfo.jsx`)

New badges added with domain-specific colors:
- **PX4-UAV** (amber) · **ZEPHYR-RTOS** (indigo) · **ROS2-ROBOTICS** (emerald) · **FEM-CFD** (red) · **RUST-CARGO** (orange) · **POETRY** (purple) · **PIPENV** (green)
- Shows **build system** and **package manager** labels next to file count

### 4. Command Resolution (`ActionButtons.jsx`)

New **Build** button (hammer icon) appears when a build system is detected. Command resolution now handles:

| Install | Build | Start |
|---|---|---|
| `pip install -r` | `colcon build --symlink-install` | `ros2 launch pkg file.launch.py` |
| `cargo build` | `west build -b qemu_cortex_m3 .` | `make px4_sitl gz_x500` |
| `poetry install` | `cargo build --release` | `west build -t run` |
| `pipenv install` | `cmake .. && make -j$(nproc)` | `cargo run` |
| `yarn install` | | `python manage.py runserver` |

### 5. Terminal Log Coloring (`Terminal.jsx`)

Real-time regex-based classification of output lines:
- **ROS2**: `[INFO]` cyan, `[WARN]` yellow, `[ERROR]` red, `[DEBUG]` gray
- **MAVLink/PX4**: amber
- **Solvers**: `converged` green bold, `diverged` red bold, iterations gray
- **Build systems**: cargo orange, cmake teal, colcon emerald, west indigo

content: Built ProjectLauncher, an Electron desktop app that AI-scans project folders and runs install/build/start commands. Just upgraded it to detect 8+ engineering project types including ROS2 robotics, PX4 drones, Zephyr firmware, and FEM simulations. Launch from your desktop shortcut and test with your D drive projects.
